texto: Most people either love or hate old libraries. To some, a room like this--dim, high-ceilinged, dusty, smelling of old paper and crumbling leather--would be oppressive, a place to flee from in search of sun and air. To others, like me, it was a wonderful cave filled with unimaginable treasures and unexpected treats. I always found myself inhaling deeply when I entered the stacks, as if trying to absorb part of them into my bloodstream.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sheila Connolly: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Most people either love or hate old libraries. To some, a room like this--dim, high-ceilinged, du…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, libraries, people pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: Most people either love or hate old libraries. To some, a room like this--dim, high-ceilinged, dusty, smelling of old paper and crumbling leather--would be oppressive, a place to flee from in search of sun and air. To others, like me, it was a wonderful cave filled with unimaginable treasures and unexpected treats. I always found myself inhaling deeply when I entered the stacks, as if trying to absorb part of them into my bloodstream.
